message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
Hi everyone
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@akshat.goel-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
is this happening in all the regions?
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Let's get this incident resolved quickly—please update us as you learn more.
We're seeing a much higher payment failure rate in Europe, but other regions are less affected so far.
@tanya can we check for any changelog in europe?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
what is the timeline of incidnet
The payment failures in Europe started about 15 minutes ago and have been ongoing since then.
*Incident Number INC1019:* payment failure sev1
I need specifics. Which regions are affected, and how many customers or transactions are impacted? Are we currently losing revenue?
The only recent changes in production within the last 15 minutes are my PaymentDatabase deployment (10 minutes ago) and Shay's CheckoutService deployment (15 minutes ago).
can we revert this
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
@tanya
I'm ready to rollback the PaymentDatabase change (CHG89350) if you want to proceed.
Alright, rolling back the PaymentDatabase change now
It's also safe to rollback my CheckoutService change (CHG89360) since there are no dependencies.
Rollback complete.
can we check the status now?
:star: Manage actions and timings
I will start checking the logs now
is it working or now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
The payment service is still returning 504 errors, so transactions are still failing.

europe is mostly affected @bez 
we are working on it..will be up within some minutes
:star: Qualify the problem
Payment service health checks continue to fail and latency remains elevated.
do we have any other changes during this time?
Yes, in addition to my PaymentDatabase change (10 minutes ago), Shay deployed a new version of CheckoutService (15 minutes ago), and Daniel deployed a PaymentService update (5 minutes ago).
related to payments
can we revert these chagnes also
Just to clarify, my PaymentService change was deployed 5 minutes ago, but the new Bitcoin payment code isn't being called yet, so it's unlikely to be the cause.
@tanya
Starting the rollback now, I'll update you shortly.
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
The PaymentDatabase change (CHG89350) is already rolled back.
Understood. I need a clear estimate of the number of impacted customers and lost transactions so far. Please confirm if revenue loss is ongoing and update me as soon as service is restored.
revert the checkoutservice then.
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
After reverting both changes, payment attempts are still failing with the same error as before.
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
right now we have reverted all the changes @tanya right?
No, Daniel's PaymentService change is still active; only the CheckoutService and PaymentDatabase changes have been rolled back so far.
can we revert this also for now?
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
@tinus ues we are working on it
Thanks for the update—let's keep the momentum and get to root cause quickly.
cool..can we checks for alerts realted to payment service?
@tanya
Checking that now...
I dug into Daniel's change and found he modified some shared libraries that are used specifically in the UK GBP payment flows, which could be impacting the failures we're seeing.
No security alerts or anomalies are showing for the payment service at this time.
can we revert this
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
@tanya
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
can we check the statgus now?
Did that fix it?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Our internal tests are all passing now. To confirm the issue is resolved for customers, let's check with Bob for the latest external metrics.
@bob please update
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
@bez business is back now.
Confirm that payments are processing successfully and revenue is being captured again. How many transactions failed during the outage?
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
yes

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/8561/feedback
I need the exact number of failed transactions and an estimate of lost revenue during the incident. Send me a summary as soon as possible.
